steps.text: Calibrate, maintain, and operate the CEMS.
Conduct CEMS performance evaluations at other times besides the initial evaluation when required by the Administrator.

preconditions.text: The CEMS must be capable of measuring the total concentrations of two or more of the specified metals in both their vapor and solid forms.

constraints.text: If a CEMS does not measure a particular metal or fails to meet the performance specifications for that metal, it may not be used to determine emission compliance for that metal.
The diluent (O2) monitor, if included, must meet its own performance specifications under 40 CFR part 60, Appendix B, Performance Specification 3.

steps.text: Calibrate, maintain, and operate the CEMS.
Conduct CEMS performance evaluations.
Conduct reference method tests to determine conformance of the CEMS with the specification.
Conduct calibration drift tests to determine conformance of the CEMS with the specification.

preconditions.text: The Administrator requires CEMS performance evaluations under Section 114 of the Act.

constraints.text: The span value shall be documented by the CEMS manufacturer with laboratory data.

steps.text: Install the CEMS at an accessible location downstream of all pollution control equipment where the metals concentrations measurements are directly representative or can be corrected so as to be representative of the total emissions from the affected facility.
Select representative measurement points or paths for monitoring in locations that the CEMS will pass the RA test.
Relocate the CEMS.

preconditions.text: The cause of failure to meet the RA test is determined to be the measurement location, a satisfactory correction technique cannot be established, and the Administrator requires relocation.

constraints.text: The measurement location should be (1) at least eight equivalent diameters downstream of the nearest control device, point of pollutant generation, bend, or other point at which a change of pollutant concentration or flow disturbance may occur and (2) at least two equivalent diameters upstream from the effluent exhaust.
The measurement point should be (1) no less than 1.0 meter from the stack or duct wall or (2) within or centrally located over the

steps.text: Calculate the equivalent duct diameter as per 40 CFR part 60, Appendix A, Method 1, Section 2.1.
Select the measurement point for Point CEMS such that it is no less than 1.0 meter from the stack or duct wall, or within/centrally located over the centroidal area.
Select traverse points to determine the representativeness of the measurement location according to 40 CFR part 60, Appendix A, Method 1, Section 2.2 and 2.3.
Establish the effective measurement path for Path CEMS according to the specified boundary/area criteria.
Select the Reference Method (RM) measurement location.
Select RM traverse measurement point locations according to 40 CFR part 60, Appendix A, Method 1, Sections 2.2 and 2.3.
Displace the RM traverse line up to 30 cm (or 5 percent of the equivalent diameter of the cross section, whichever is less) from the centroidal area.

preconditions.text: The RM traverse line interferes with or is interfered by the CEMS measurements.

constraints.text: The RM and CEMS locations should be as close as possible without causing interference with one another to prevent failure of the CEMS to pass the RA test.

The RM measurement location must be at least eight equivalent diameters downstream of the nearest control device, point of pollutant generation, bend, or other flow disturbance, and at least two equivalent diameters upstream from the effluent exhaust.

steps.text: Perform calibration at a point equal to 80 to 120 percent of the applicable emission standard for each metal.
Determine calibration drift at the zero level (zero drift) for each metal.
Determine the zero drift at a low level value (zero to 20 percent of the emission limit value).
Sample the stack effluent continuously.

preconditions.text: If determining calibration drift at the zero level is not possible or practicable

constraints.text: The CEMS data recorder response range must include zero and a high level value equal to the span value (or automatically adjust so measured value does not exceed 95 percent of the high level value).

The RA of the CEMS must be no greater than 20 percent of the mean value of the RM test data in terms of units of the emission standard for each metal, or 10 percent of the applicable standard, whichever is greater.
The CEMS calibration may not drift or deviate from the reference value (RV) of the calibration standard used for each metal by more than 5 percent of the emission standard for each metal.
The CEMS zero point for each metal shall not drift by more than 5 percent of the emission standard for that metal.

steps.text: Determine the magnitude of the calibration drift (CD) and zero drift (ZD) once each day (at 24-hour intervals) for 7 consecutive days according to the procedure given in Section 6.
Conduct a RA test according to the procedure given in Section 7.
Conduct the CD test immediately before periodic automatic or manual adjustments, or conduct it in such a way that the CD and ZD can be determined.
Conduct the CD and ZD tests at the points specified in Sections 4.3 and 4.4.
Record the CEMS response and calculate the CD and ZD according to the formulas.

preconditions.text: The affected facility is operating at more than 50 percent of normal load, or as specified in an applicable subpart.
A 24-hour period has passed during which no unscheduled maintenance, repair, or manual adjustment of the CEMS took place.
Periodic automatic or manual adjustments are made to the CEMS zero and calibration settings.

constraints.text: None of the CD’s or ZD’s may exceed the specification to meet the requirements of Sections 4.3 and 4.4.

steps.text: Calculate the ZD according to Equation (2).
Conduct the RM measurements in such a way that they will yield results representative of the emissions from the source and can be correlated to the CEMS data.
Determine the RA at three levels (0 to 20, 40 to 60, and 80 to 120 percent of the emission limit) for one of the metals which will be monitored, or for iron.
Note the beginning and end of each RM test period of each run (including the exact time of day) in the CEMS data log.

preconditions.text: If iron is chosen, the three levels should be chosen to correspond to those for one of the metals that will be monitored using known sensitivities (documented by the manufacturer) or the CEMS to both metals.
The concentration of each metal must be detectable by both the CEMS and the RM.

constraints.text: Conduct the diluent (if applicable), moisture (if needed), and pollutant measurements simultaneously, or use diluent and moisture measurements taken within a 30- to 60-minute period that includes the pollutant measurements.

steps.text: Summarize the results on a data sheet.
Calculate the mean of the RM values.
Calculate the arithmetic differences between the RM and CEMS output sets.
Calculate the mean of the differences.
Calculate the standard deviation of each data set and CEMS RA using the equations in Section 8.
Spike the appropriate metals in the feed or operate the facility in such a way as to raise the metal concentration to a level detectable by both the CEMS and the RM.
Calculate the arithmetic mean of a data set using Equation 3.
Calculate the arithmetic mean of the difference, d, of a data set, using Equation 3 and substituting d for x.
Calculate the standard deviation (SD) of a data set using Equation 5.
Calculate the Relative Accuracy (RA) using Equation 6.

preconditions.text: Metals emissions concentrations from the source are so low as to be undetectable by the CEMS operating in its normal mode.

steps.text: Summarize in tabular form the results of the CD tests and the RA tests or alternate RA procedure as appropriate.
Include all data sheets, calculations, and records of CEMS response necessary to substantiate that the performance of the CEMS met the performance specifications.
Report the CEMS measurements to the agency in units of µg/m³ on a dry basis, corrected to 20°C or 7 percent O2.

preconditions.text: Check with the appropriate regional office, State, or local agency for additional requirements.
